title: richardia montana
intro: Richardia montana, also known as the Hawaiian mallow or stinging plant, is a species of flowering plant native to Hawaii. It is characterized by its small, yellow flowers and leaves covered with fine, irritating hairs that can cause skin irritation in some individuals. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other infections, and promote overall health and well-being.
